verdict.answer_first: B states a specific, actionable recommendation in its first sentence ('Use Postgres's built-in full-text search'), while A states a general principle ('Use your existing database's built-in full-text search') that requires further decision-making.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to a single plan for the most likely case ('I'll give you the plan for the most likely case — Postgres'), whereas A presents a menu of options ('pick your branch').
verdict.fabrication: B invents context the user never gave: 'Your global context leans heavily on Aurora PostgreSQL, so that's my working assumption.'
verdict.actionability: B is immediately actionable because it provides concrete, copy-pasteable SQL code for the migration and query. A provides a conceptual plan that requires another round of questions or research.
